summary: The service is structurally sound, but I found three meaningful correctness issues in the hot path: sitepull capture resolution is too fragile, clone-session allocation can leak live Vite servers on post-allocation failures, and port selection is racy under concurrent `/clone` requests. SSE handling is mostly acceptable on `/clone` and `/edit`, but `/clone/stub` still drops mid-stream failures without emitting an error event. The Zod gate and JSON Schema agree on required fields and enums, but they drift on permissiveness and extra-field handling.

issues.description: `SitepullResolver` matches roots by `entry.includes(host)` and then only checks `root/<entry>/anatomy.json` or `root/<entry>/<child>/anatomy.json`. That is both lossy and ambiguous: local `.tmp` captures are often named arbitrarily (`hn-v1`, `example-v1`, etc.), so valid captures are skipped entirely, while substring matching can also pick unrelated hosts (for example `notexample.com` for `example
issues.suggested_fix: Stop using substring directory-name matching as the primary key. Either read candidate `anatomy.json` metadata and compare `new URL(metadata.target).hostname` exactly (with explicit host canonicalization rules), or maintain a host-indexed capture manifest. Keep root-specific traversal logic: `.canvas/<host>/<slug>/anatomy.json`, `.tmp/**/anatomy.json`, and `audits/<host>/anatomy.json`.

issues.description: `runUrlToClone()` allocates a Vite session and then writes generated files. If `writeFiles()` fails after allocation succeeds, the catch block returns an SSE error but never releases the session. That leaves a live Vite server, occupied port, and orphaned session directory until manual cleanup or process shutdown.
issues.suggested_fix: Wrap the post-allocation phase in a nested `try/catch` that calls `await deps.pool.release(session.sessionId)` before yielding the error, or add an atomic `allocateAndSeed()` API on `VitePool` so allocation is rolled back automatically when seeding fails.

issues.description: Port allocation is race-prone under concurrent `/clone` requests. Multiple callers can observe the same lowest free port before either inserts into `this.sessions`. The code retries only once, again using the still-stale `sessions` map, so 3+ concurrent allocations can fail spuriously with `EADDRINUSE` even when free ports remain in `3060-3069`.
issues.suggested_fix: Reserve ports before awaiting `startServer()` or guard allocation with a mutex/semaphore. At minimum, retry in a loop across the whole range while tracking ports currently being started, not just ports already present in `this.sessions`.

issues.description: `/clone/stub` does not catch generator failures. If `generateStub()` throws after some SSE frames have already been sent, the handler just falls into `finally { res.end(); }`, so the client gets an abrupt EOF instead of an `error` event. `/clone` and `/edit` do emit explicit error events; `/clone/stub` is inconsistent.
issues.suggested_fix: Mirror the `/clone` and `/edit` handlers: catch generator errors, emit a final SSE `{ type: 'error', ... }` event when possible, then close the stream.

issues.description: The Zod gate and `contracts/schemas/AnatomyV1.v1.json` agree on required fields and enum values, but they drift on permissiveness. JSON Schema allows arbitrary extra properties on `region`, `chainNode`, and `layers`, while Zod strips unknown properties by default and requires any extra `layers.*` value to match `{ color: string }`. That can silently drop producer data or reject payloads that the `
issues.suggested_fix: Decide whether the runtime contract should be strict or passthrough, then align both schemas. If passthrough is intended, use `.passthrough()` on `regionSchema`/`chainNodeSchema` and relax `layersTaxonomySchema`; if strict is intended, tighten the JSON Schema with `additionalProperties: false` (or typed additionalProperties) to match.

strengths: `targetId` from the request body is not used directly as a filesystem path; edit writes go through `VitePool.resolveSessionPath()` and component filenames are synthesized from sanitized region names,`
Normal graceful shutdown is implemented: `server.ts` calls `pool.shutdown()` on `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM`, and `VitePool.release()` removes both the dev server and session directory.
The required-field and enum portions of the in-process Zod schema and the sibling JSON Schema are aligned; the schema drift is about extra-property handling, not core field presence.
